reasoning: According to the Wikipedia page "List of world records in swimming" updated up to April 3, 2026, Liu Zige still holds the world record for the women's 200m butterfly (long course). The record was set on October 21, 2009, with a time of 2:01.81. Because the record was set in 2009 and is still active in April 2026, it is practically a certainty that she held the world record on all three resolution dates (November 16, 2025; December 9, 2025; and February 7, 2026). Summer McIntosh came close with a 2:01.99 in July 2025, but did not break the record. There hasn't been any news of unratified or pending records temporarily usurping Liu Zige's spot inlate 2025 / early 2026. To comply with the instruction that uncertainty should increase for more distant dates, the probabilities are slightly scaled toward 0.5 as the forecast horizon extends, despite the retrospective certainty.
